Sources.Title: The Proof with Simon Hill — My Biological Age (And the Surprising Thing I Found in My Bloodwork), Episode 373
The Proof — Simon Hill's 2025 blood-test results

TranscriptionNotes: The subject-published Function Health walkthrough and linked report visibly provide all nine stored biomarkers from the same May 2025 panel.

The dashboard directly displays a calendar age of 38.9 years and states that calendar age is based on the date of the lab visit. AgeYears is the displayed calendar age; it was not derived from the displayed biological-age difference.
The source identifies the lab visit at month precision as May 2025. The stored date uses 1 May 2025 only as the required representation of that month-precision source; no specimen day was inferred.
The source reports hs-CRP as below 0.2 mg/L. The stored value is the published assay limit and the less-than qualifier is preserved; it is not presented as an exact measured concentration.
Albumin was converted from g/dL to g/L by multiplying by 10.
Creatinine was converted from mg/dL to µmol/L by multiplying by 88.4.
Glucose was converted from mg/dL to mmol/L by multiplying by 0.0555.
